feed.title: Google Cloud: App Engine standard environment Node.js
feed.subtitle: Changelog for Google Cloud product: App Engine standard environment Node.js

entry.content: App Engine supports Direct VPC egress in Preview. Direct VPC egress lets your workloads access VPC network resources as a simpler, more cost-effective alternative to Serverless VPC Access connectors.
entry.summary: App Engine now supports Direct VPC egress in preview. This feature allows your workloads to access VPC network resources, offering a simpler and more cost-effective alternative to Serverless VPC Access connectors.
entry.title: Feature: App Engine adds Direct VPC Egress

entry.content: The App Engine Migration hub lets you migrate services in the App Engine standard environment to Cloud Run, and also provides cost-saving recommendations. For more information, see Deploy an App Engine app in the standard environment to Cloud Run (Preview).
entry.summary: Easily migrate your App Engine standard environment services to Cloud Run. This new feature also offers recommendations to help you save costs on your applications.
entry.title: Feature: App Engine Migration to Cloud Run

entry.content: Support for deploying your existing apps in the standard environment to Cloud Run using the gcloud beta app migrate-to-run command is in Preview. For more information, see Deploy an App Engine app in the standard environment to Cloud Run.
entry.summary: You can now migrate your existing App Engine standard apps to Cloud Run using the `gcloud beta app migrate-to-run` command. This feature is currently in Preview and offers a streamlined path to modernize your applications.
entry.title: Feature: Migrate App Engine standard to Cloud Run

entry.summary: The Node.js 24 runtime is now generally available for use.
entry.title: Feature: Node.js 24 Runtime Now Generally Available

entry.content: To increase security, starting in March 2025, support for Transport Layer Security (TLS) version 1.1 and earlier is deprecated. Update your application settings in the App Engine standard environment to use TLS version 1.2 and later, along with a corresponding secure set of cipher suites (Preview).
entry.summary: TLS 1.2+ support is required starting March 2025.
entry.title: Feature: TLS 1.1 and earlier support deprecated

entry.content: Support for Node.js 24 runtime is in Preview. Node.js 24 is in the Current release state and enters long-term support (LTS) in October 2025. For more information, see Node.js v24.0.0 (Current) in the Node.js website.
entry.summary: Node.js 24 runtime now available in preview.
entry.title: Feature: Preview: Node.js 24 Runtime
